title: Leucheria glabriuscula
intro: Leucheria glabriuscula, also known as Leucheria, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be used to treat various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Leucheria glabriuscula include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Leucheria is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, and it is also used in traditional medicine in some parts of the world.
taxonomy: Leucheria glabriuscula is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is native to New Zealand and is a member of the Brassicaceae family, which includes other plants like broccoli and cauliflower. The family Brassicaceae is further classified into the order Brassicales. The order Brassicales is a part of the subclass Rosidae, which is a subcategory of the class Magnoliopsida. Within the subclass Rosidae, the class Magnoliopsida is a group of flowering plants that have two cotyledons in the seedling stage.
